lexis 0.1.0
  • Reference

Look up psycholinguistic norms for one or more words

Source: R/lookup.R
lex_lookup.Rd

Returns a tidy (long) tibble of norm values for the requested words. By default returns all available dimensions; use dims to restrict.

lex_lookup(words, dims = NULL, wide = FALSE)

Arguments

words

character vector. One or more words to look up (case-insensitive).

dims

character vector or NULL. Dimensions to return. If NULL, all dimensions are returned. See lexis_meta$dimension for valid names.

wide

logical. If TRUE, pivot to wide format (one row per word). Default FALSE (long format).

Value

A tibble. In long format: word, lemma, dataset, dimension, mean, sd, n_ratings, scale_min, scale_max. In wide format: word, lemma, then one column per dimension.

Examples

lex_lookup("dog")
#> # A tibble: 27 × 9
#>    word  lemma dataset      dimension   mean    sd n_ratings scale_min scale_max
#>    <chr> <chr> <chr>        <chr>      <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>
#>  1 dog   dog   aoa          aoa        2.8   1.2          20         1        25
#>  2 dog   dog   boi          boi        6.4   1.26         25         1         7
#>  3 dog   dog   concreteness concreten… 4.85  0.46         27         1         5
#>  4 dog   dog   iconicity    iconicity  2.5   1.90         10         1         7
#>  5 dog   dog   lancaster    lancaster… 4.12  1.27         17         0         5
#>  6 dog   dog   lancaster    lancaster… 0.588 1.18         17         0         5
#>  7 dog   dog   lancaster    lancaster… 3.82  1.51         17         0         5
#>  8 dog   dog   lancaster    lancaster… 0.647 1.32         17         0         5
#>  9 dog   dog   lancaster    lancaster… 2.82  1.91         17         0         5
#> 10 dog   dog   lancaster    lancaster… 4.59  0.870        17         0         5
#> # ℹ 17 more rows
lex_lookup(c("dog", "cat"), dims = c("aoa", "concreteness", "valence"))
#> # A tibble: 6 × 9
#>   word  lemma dataset      dimension    mean    sd n_ratings scale_min scale_max
#>   <chr> <chr> <chr>        <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>
#> 1 dog   dog   aoa          aoa          2.8   1.2         20         1        25
#> 2 cat   cat   aoa          aoa          3.68  1.52        22         1        25
#> 3 cat   cat   concreteness concretene…  4.86  0.52        29         1         5
#> 4 dog   dog   concreteness concretene…  4.85  0.46        27         1         5
#> 5 cat   cat   warriner_vad valence      6.95  1.85        20         1         9
#> 6 dog   dog   warriner_vad valence      7     2.07        42         1         9
lex_lookup("run", wide = TRUE)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 29
#>   word  lemma   aoa   boi concreteness iconicity lancaster_auditory
#>   <chr> <chr> <dbl> <dbl>        <dbl>     <dbl>              <dbl>
#> 1 run   run    4.47  3.22         4.31       3.8               1.61
#> # ℹ 22 more variables: lancaster_gustatory <dbl>, lancaster_haptic <dbl>,
#> #   lancaster_interoceptive <dbl>, lancaster_olfactory <dbl>,
#> #   lancaster_visual <dbl>, lancaster_foot_leg <dbl>, lancaster_hand_arm <dbl>,
#> #   lancaster_head <dbl>, lancaster_mouth <dbl>, lancaster_torso <dbl>,
#> #   valence <dbl>, arousal <dbl>, dominance <dbl>, glasgow_valence <dbl>,
#> #   glasgow_arousal <dbl>, glasgow_dominance <dbl>, glasgow_concreteness <dbl>,
#> #   glasgow_imageability <dbl>, glasgow_familiarity <dbl>, glasgow_aoa <dbl>, …
